title: Preparation and transformation of electrocompetent cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"46mg2","text":"A protocol for electroporation of E. coli. Other bacteria may work, with optimization of transformation buffer and settings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In a sterile flask, add 400 mL distilled water

Inoculate 500 mL of prewarmed LB medium from 25 mL overnight E. coli culture. Incubate at 37 °C and 300 rpm. Measure OD every 20 minutes, until OD 600 =0.4 The density is usually archived after ~2.5 hours of incubation with DH5α
Pre-chill cuvettes on ice for 00:05:00 , and pre-heat LB plates with an appropriate selection agent and SOC medium at 37 °C for 01:00:00

Measure and add 0.6 g yeast extract, 1.25 g tryptone, and 50 mL glycerol Materials: Select Yeast Extract Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #Y0875 Tryptone Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #T9410 (CAS: 91079-40-2) Glycerol MP Biomedicals Catalog #194680 (CAS: 56-81-5)
Centrifuge culture at 1000 rcf, 4 °C, 20 minutes , and resuspend pellet in 100 mL ice-cold 10% glycerol
Centrifuge culture at 1000 rcf, 4 °C, 15 minutes , and resuspend pellet in 200 mL ice-cold water Combine the culture into a smaller number of centrifugation containers
Transfer the culture to appropriate centrifugation containers, and cool on ice for 00:30:00
Plate an appropriate amount culture on selection agar plates
Flush 1 mL pre-heated S. O. C. medium, then transfer to a culture tube and recover at 37 °C and 200 rpm shaking for 01:00:00
Incubate plasmid-suspension mix for 1 minute, then perform electroporation with optimized settings
Filter sterilize through a 0.22-µm filter, and store in aliquots at 4 °C
Measure OD 600 of a 1:100 dilution of the cell suspension, and dilute the cells to between 2-3 × 10 10 cells/mL 1.0 OD 600 =~2.5 X 10 8 cells/ml
Centrifuge culture at 1000 rcf, 4 °C, 20 minutes , and resuspend pellet in 2 mL ice-cold GYT medium This is best done by gentle swirling rather than pipetting or vortexing
Centrifuge culture at 1000 rcf, 4 °C, 20 minutes , and resuspend pellet in 25 mL ice-cold 10% glycerol

Transfer 40 µl of the suspension to an ice-chilled electroporation cuvette and test whether arching occurs when an electrical discharge is applied. If so, wash the remainder of the cell suspension once more with ice-cold GYT medium to ensure that the conductivity of the bacterial suspension is sufficiently low
Dispense 40 µL aliquots of the cell suspension into sterile, ice-cold microfuge , drop into a bath of liquid nitrogen, and transfer to a -80 °C freezer

Mix an appropriate amount plasmid to an aliquot of electrocompetent cells and transfer to a pre-chilled cuvette 100 pg pUC19 plasmid is appropriate in most cases, but a specific amount plasmid depend on many different factors and have to be optimized S. O. C. medium by Andreas Sagen
creator: Andreas Sagen
